extension-point.name: event
extension-point.documentation:
This service registers auditable events.
Registered events are dummy strings for now.
This service is used to filter auditable events from the JMS topic based on their names. The following XML snipset give figures out how the default event types are selected for auditing.
@deprecated since 2025.16, use routes + extendedInfo extension points instead

extension-point.name: routes
extension-point.documentation:
Allows to register audit routes for log entry backend storage location.
A route is basically composed of a backend name and a list of events to forward to this backend.
Such routes also register the events to convert to log entries that will later be routed to backends.
A route can also be composed of a backend name and a list of predicates.
Such routes don't register events to convert to log entries, they only act at backend routing layer.
eventDocumentCategory
The events and predicates can be combined, they are computed as AND when routing log entries to backends. The OR combination is achieved by declaring several routes with the same backend. Keep in mind that event conversion is only done for events registered in routes.

extension-point.name: extendedInfo
extension-point.documentation:
This service registered extended info mappings.
This service is used to evaluate EL expression using document as context registering results into a map indexed by names.

extension-point.name: adapter
extension-point.documentation: register the adapter that will be injected in EL context

extension-point.name: backendFactory
extension-point.documentation: Allows to register a backend implementation for the Audit Service
